framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Lead a full build-test-fly hardware team as a student
paths.what_it_is: Take a leadership role - structures, propulsion, or chief engineer - on a student rocketry or UAV team that designs, builds, tests, and flies real hardware against a hard competition deadline.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can own a hardware subsystem end to end under real schedule and safety constraints, exactly the judgment employers are betting on when they sponsor a new grad.

paths.path: Build and maintain an open-source aerospace engineering tool
paths.what_it_is: Write and sustain a public simulation or design-optimization tool - a trajectory simulator or aerostructural optimization framework - from a student project into an actively maintained release.
paths.why_it_signals: Shipping software other engineers trust with real flight and design decisions proves rigor, documentation discipline, and technical range beyond what a single class project or internship can show.

paths.path: Translate aerospace engineering into public teaching content
paths.what_it_is: Hold a real aerospace or aeronautical engineering credential while running a public-facing channel, blog, or video series that explains real engineering concepts, builds, and test data to a general audience.
paths.why_it_signals: Consistently explaining technical material clearly, backed by genuine engineering work, proves a depth of understanding and communication skill that credentials alone can't show.
